steps.title: Support grass-not-trees claim
steps.content: Martinez and colleagues concluded that the dam's changed flow benefited grasses but did not benefit trees. The graph evidence should therefore show grass cover increasing while forest cover fails to increase.

steps.title: Pick the contrasting trend
steps.content: A gives isolated extremes, B compares grass with forest in each year, and C compares each cover type with bare soil. The data that best support the conclusion show grass cover rising from 1987 to 1993 to 2006 while forest cover falls. The answer is D.
